codeReview.summary: The concert-tab implementation is solid and meets all feature requirements. The Concert page at app/concert/page.tsx renders a search bar ('Find a star or performance'), banner image, section header with Music icon, and concert event cards showing artist photos, event names, dates, venues, and prices. ConcertCard component at components/concert/ConcertCard.tsx is well-structured with proper formatting utilities for date and price. Search filtering works by both artist name and event title. Empty state displays 'No concerts found'. Cards link correctly to /events/[id]. The implementation follows the same patterns as football and basketball tabs consistently. 15 tests cover ConcertCard (6 tests) and ConcertPage (9 tests) including rendering, search filtering by artist/event name, empty state, loading state, and event type isolation. The @testing-library/user-event package was installed as a dev dependency for interactive search tests.

issues.description: When fetch fails (catch block), the error is only logged to console. The user sees 'No concerts found' (empty state) instead of a meaningful error message. This is consistent with the football and basketball pages which have the same pattern, so it's a project-wide concern rather than a concert-specific bug.

The loading spinner div uses role='status' which is an accessibility improvement over the football and basketball pages (which have no role on their spinners). This inconsistency is minor and actually represents better practice — the other tabs could benefit from this pattern too.

Test uses getByRole('status', { hidden: true }) which passes { hidden: true } to find the spinner element. This option tells testing-library to include elements normally hidden from accessibility tree. While it works, it suggests the spinner's role may not be properly discoverable. A minor concern — the pattern is functional.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker installed @testing-library/user-event as a new dev dependency. This is a legitimate testing utility for simulating user interactions (typing in search fields). AGENTS.md does not mention this library, but it's a standard companion to @testing-library/react. Currently only used in concert tests — future features with interactive test scenarios (forms, search) would also benefit from it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json line 26 shows '@testing-library/user-event': '^14.6.1' added. Only imported in __tests__/components/concert/ConcertPage.test.tsx.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The concert page adds role='status' to the loading spinner (line 96), while the football page (app/football/page.tsx) and basketball page (app/basketball/page.tsx) do not add any ARIA role to their identical spinner patterns. This is an inconsistency where the concert implementation is actually better for accessibility, but the convention is not documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/concert/page.tsx:96 has role='status', app/football/page.tsx and app/basketball/page.tsx both use plain div with no role for identical spinner markup.

sharedStateObservations.observation: All three event tab pages (football, basketball, concert) share the exact same fetch-filter-render pattern with no error state UI — errors are logged to console and user sees 'No [events] found' instead of an error message. This is a consistent project-wide pattern but could be documented as a known limitation or convention in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/concert/page.tsx:34-35, app/football/page.tsx (same pattern), app/basketball/page.tsx (same pattern) — all catch blocks only console.error and let isLoading become false with empty events array.

summary: Concert tab implementation passes review. All feature requirements are met: search bar with 'Find a star or performance' placeholder, banner image, concert event cards with artist photos/names/dates/venues/prices, clickable cards linking to /events/[id], real-time search filtering by artist name and event title, and empty state. The code follows established patterns from football/basketball tabs with good consistency. 15 tests provide solid coverage. Three non-blocking issues noted: (1) no user-facing error state on fetch failure (consistent with other tabs), (2) minor accessibility improvement over other tabs with role='status' on spinner (inconsistency, but better practice), (3) test uses { hidden: true } option for spinner role query. None are blocking.
